_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning how choosing the right verb, using elaborated tenses and including different types of adverb groups can sharpen and expand our ideas.

_source.w: We are learning how choosing the right verb, using elaborated tenses and including different types of adverb groups can sharpen and expand our ideas.
y: Precise verb choices and expanded verb groups make our writing more vivid and accurate.
Understanding elaborated tenses — like 'had been running' — gives us tools for expressing complex time relationships.
These grammatical tools help us write with the precision and sophistication expected at this level.
eg: We compare 'walked' with 'strolled', 'marched' and 'wandered' and discuss the different images each creates.
We explore elaborated tenses — 'She was running' versus 'She had been running' — and discuss the difference in meaning.
We add adverb groups to sentences to sharpen the meaning — 'He spoke quietly and carefully.'
We analyse how an author's verb choices create a particular tone or mood.
We edit our writing to replace vague verbs with more precise ones.
We practise using a range of adverb groups to add detail about how, when, where and why.
